<content>1: package main

3: import (
4: 	"fmt"
5: 	"log"
6: 	"os"
7: 	"time"
8: 
9: 	"clubops_portal/fullstack/internal/handlers"
10: 	"clubops_portal/fullstack/internal/middleware"
11: 	"clubops_portal/fullstack/internal/services"
12: 	"clubops_portal/fullstack/internal/store"
13: 
14: 	"github.com/gofiber/fiber/v2"
15: 	"github.com/gofiber/fiber/v2/middleware/logger"
16: 	"github.com/gofiber/fiber/v2/middleware/recover"
17: 	"github.com/gofiber/template/html/v2"
18: )
19: 
20: func main() {
21: 	dbPath := os.Getenv("APP_DB_PATH")
22: 	if dbPath == "" {
23: 		dbPath = "./fullstack.db"
24: 	}
25: 	port := os.Getenv("APP_PORT")
26: 	if port == "" {
27: 		port = "8080"
28: 	}
29: 	logEvent("startup", "info", "opening sqlite at %s", dbPath)
30: 	st, err := store.NewSQLiteStore(dbPath)
31: 	if err != nil {
32: 		logEvent("startup", "fatal", "open db: %v", err)
33: 		log.Fatalf("open db: %v", err)
34: 	}
35: 	defer st.Close()
36: 
37: 	if err := st.AutoMigrate(); err != nil {
38: 		logEvent("startup", "fatal", "migrate db: %v", err)
39: 		log.Fatalf("migrate db: %v", err)
40: 	}
41: 	if err := st.SeedDefaults(); err != nil {
42: 		logEvent("startup", "fatal", "seed defaults: %v", err)
43: 		log.Fatalf("seed defaults: %v", err)
44: 	}
45: 
46: 	authSvc := services.NewAuthService(st, 30*time.Minute, 5, 15*time.Minute)
47: 	financeSvc := services.NewFinanceService(st)
48: 	creditSvc := services.NewCreditService(st)
49: 	reviewSvc := services.NewReviewService(st, "./fullstack/static/uploads")
50: 	mdmSvc := services.NewMDMService(st)
51: 	auditSvc := services.NewAuditService(st)
52: 	flagSvc := services.NewFlagService(st)
53: 	cryptoSvc, err := services.NewCryptoService()
54: 	if err != nil {
55: 		logEvent("startup", "fatal", "init crypto service: %v", err)
56: 		log.Fatalf("init crypto service: %v", err)
57: 	}
58: 
59: 	logEvent("worker", "info", "starting threshold and audit workers")
60: 	go financeSvc.StartThresholdWorker(60 * time.Second)
61: 	go auditSvc.StartRetentionWorker(24 * time.Hour)
62: 
63: 	engine := html.New("./fullstack/views", ".html")
64: 	app := fiber.New(fiber.Config{Views: engine, BodyLimit: 15 * 1024 * 1024})
65: 	app.Use(recover.New())
66: 	app.Use(logger.New(logger.Config{
67: 		Format:     "category=http_request level=info method=${method} path=${path} status=${status} latency=${latency} ip=${ip}\n",
68: 		TimeFormat: time.RFC3339,
69: 	}))
70: 	app.Use(middleware.CSRFProtection())
71: 	app.Use(middleware.AttachCurrentUser(authSvc))
72: 	app.Use(middleware.AuditTrail(auditSvc, st))
73: 
74: 	h := handlers.NewHandler(st, authSvc, financeSvc, creditSvc, reviewSvc, mdmSvc, cryptoSvc, flagSvc)
75: 	h.RegisterRoutes(app)

<type>file</type>
<content>1: # ClubOps Governance & Finance Portal
2: 
3: Go (Fiber) + HTMX + SQLite implementation for multi-site membership governance, moderation, and finance workflows.
4: 
5: ## One-click start
6: 
7: ```bash
8: docker compose -f fullstack/docker-compose.yml up --build
9: ```
10: 
11: App URL: `http://localhost:8080`
12: 
13: Required env:
14: - `APP_ENCRYPTION_KEY` (set in `docker-compose.yml` for local default)
15: - `APP_DB_PATH` (defaults to `./fullstack.db` for local runs; Docker sets `/data/fullstack.db`)
16: - `APP_BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_PASSWORD` (required when seeding the admin account)
17: - `APP_PORT` (optional; defaults to `8080`)
18: 
19: Persistence:
20: - SQLite DB in Docker volume: `sqlite_data`
21: - Uploaded review images in: `./fullstack/static/uploads`
22: 
23: ## Seeded account
24: 
25: - Username: `admin`
26: - Initial password: value of `APP_BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_PASSWORD`
27: 
28: The seeded account is configured for forced password change/reset workflow; use:
29: - `POST /api/auth/change-password` (for current user)
30: - `POST /api/auth/admin-reset` (admin-only)
31: 
32: When a user is flagged for password rotation (`must_change_password = 1`), the UI redirects to `/change-password` until the password is updated.
33: 
34: Registration:
35: - Public `/register` creates `member` accounts only.
36: - Elevated roles (`team_lead`, `organizer`, `admin`) must be assigned by admin workflows.
37: 
38: ## Test commands
39: 
40: ```bash
41: ./run_tests.sh
42: ```
43: 
44: Equivalent:
45: 
46: ```bash
47: go test ./fullstack/unit_tests/... -v
48: go test ./fullstack/API_tests/... -v
49: ```
50: 
51: Optional browser E2E (Playwright):
52: 
53: ```bash
54: cd fullstack/e2e
55: npm install
56: npm run test:smoke
57: E2E_ADMIN_USER=admin E2E_ADMIN_PASS=your-admin-password npm run test:auth
58: ```
59: 
60: Deterministic local profile:
61: 
62: - `test:smoke` validates baseline unauthenticated UI rendering.
63: - `test:auth` requires `E2E_ADMIN_USER` and `E2E_ADMIN_PASS` and runs authenticated end-to-end flows.
64: - CI should run `test:auth` with seeded credentials to prevent silent downgrade to smoke-only verification.
65: 
66: ## Local (non-Docker) run
67: 
68: ```bash
69: export APP_ENCRYPTION_KEY=local-dev-encryption-key-please-change
70: export APP_DB_PATH=./fullstack.db
71: export APP_BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_PASSWORD=change-this-bootstrap-admin-pass
72: export APP_PORT=8080
73: go run ./fullstack/cmd
74: ```
75: 
76: Windows PowerShell:
77: 
78: ```powershell
79: $env:APP_ENCRYPTION_KEY="local-dev-encryption-key-please-change"
80: $env:APP_DB_PATH="./fullstack.db"
81: $env:APP_BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_PASSWORD="change-this-bootstrap-admin-pass"
82: $env:APP_PORT="8080"
83: go run ./fullstack/cmd
84: ```
85: 
86: ## Security notes
87: 
88: - bcrypt cost `12`
89: - Session timeout `30m` with sliding refresh on active use
90: - 5 failed login attempts lock account for 15 minutes
91: - Club-scoped access for non-admin operators with assigned clubs
92: - Object-level checks on budget/review mutations
93: - CSRF protection on authenticated mutations via `csrf_token` cookie + request token
94: - App-layer encryption for member contact fields (AES-GCM)
95: - `members.custom_fields` is stored encrypted at rest with an `enc:v1:` prefix; plaintext legacy rows are lazily migrated on read/write paths
96: - Audit logs are append-only for mutation endpoints and have 2-year retention cleanup
97: - Audit payload capture is path-allowlisted; sensitive fields (`email`, `phone`, `custom_fields`, `comment`, auth tokens/passwords, identifiers) are redacted by default
98: - Frontend runtime assets are served locally from `/static/vendor` for offline use
99: 
100: ## Sensitive field policy
101: 
102: - Member PII (`email_encrypted`, `phone_encrypted`) and `custom_fields` must only be persisted in encrypted form
103: - New schema additions carrying identifiers or contact data should follow the same app-layer encryption approach and audit redaction policy
104: 
105: ## API error schema
106: 
107: - API endpoints return a normalized error shape: `{ "error": "...", "error_code": "...", "message": "..." }`
108: - `error_code` values include: `validation_error`, `conflict`, `forbidden`, `not_found`, `bad_request`
109: - Detailed internal errors are logged server-side; client responses are sanitized for safety and consistency
110: - `APP_DEBUG_ERRORS` should remain disabled outside local development to avoid verbose raw error details in logs
111: 
112: ## Additional workflows
113: 
114: - Reviews must reference a fulfilled order/service record (`fulfilled_order_id`)
115: - Region hierarchy management is available at `/regions` for admin/organizer users
116: - Dimension and sales fact import workflows are available at `/mdm`
117: - Admin user provisioning/role assignment is available at `/users`
118: - Club avatars can be uploaded locally and are stored under `./fullstack/static/uploads/avatars`
119: - Budget spend updates can be recorded from `/budgets` to drive execution and alerting
120: - Member CSV import returns an inline downloadable error-report link in the members UI when row validation fails

(End of file - total 120 lines)
</content>

13: func saveAvatarFile(fh *multipart.FileHeader) (string, error) {
14: 	ext := strings.ToLower(filepath.Ext(fh.Filename))
15: 	if ext != ".jpg" && ext != ".jpeg" && ext != ".png" {
16: 		return "", errors.New("avatar must be jpg or png")
17: 	}
18: 	if fh.Size > 2*1024*1024 {
19: 		return "", errors.New("avatar must be <= 2MB")
20: 	}
21: 	dir := filepath.Join(".", "static", "uploads", "avatars")
22: 	if err := os.MkdirAll(dir, 0o755); err != nil {
23: 		return "", err
24: 	}
25: 	name := strconv.FormatInt(time.Now().UnixNano(), 10) + "_" + filepath.Base(fh.Filename)
26: 	target := filepath.Join(dir, name)
27: 	src, err := fh.Open()
28: 	if err != nil {
29: 		return "", err
30: 	}
31: 	defer src.Close()
32: 	out, err := os.Create(target)
33: 	if err != nil {
34: 		return "", err
35: 	}
36: 	defer out.Close()
37: 	if _, err := out.ReadFrom(src); err != nil {
38: 		return "", err
39: 	}
40: 	return "/static/uploads/avatars/" + name, nil
41: }
42: 
43: func saveMemberImportErrorReport(contents string) (string, error) {
44: 	dir := filepath.Join(".", "static", "uploads", "reports")
45: 	if err := os.MkdirAll(dir, 0o755); err != nil {
46: 		return "", err
47: 	}
48: 	name := "member_import_errors_" + strconv.FormatInt(time.Now().UnixNano(), 10) + ".csv"
49: 	target := filepath.Join(dir, name)
50: 	if err := os.WriteFile(target, []byte(contents), 0o644); err != nil {
51: 		return "", err
52: 	}
53: 	return "/static/uploads/reports/" + name, nil
54: }

      dockerfile: Dockerfile

18: 	return &FinanceService{store: st}
19: }

21: func (s *FinanceService) CreateBudget(clubID int64, accountCode, campusCode, projectCode, periodType, periodStart string, amount float64, userID int64) (int64, error) {
22: 	if amount <= 0 {
23: 		return 0, errors.New("amount must be positive")
24: 	}
25: 	if periodType != "monthly" && periodType != "quarterly" {
26: 		return 0, errors.New("period_type must be monthly or quarterly")
27: 	}
28: 	return s.store.InsertBudget(models.Budget{
29: 		ClubID:      clubID,
30: 		AccountCode: accountCode,
31: 		CampusCode:  campusCode,
32: 		ProjectCode: projectCode,
33: 		PeriodType:  periodType,
34: 		PeriodStart: periodStart,
35: 		Amount:      amount,
36: 		Spent:       0,
37: 		CreatedBy:   userID,
38: 		Status:      "active",
39: 	})
40: }
41: 
42: func (s *FinanceService) RequestBudgetChange(budgetID, requesterID int64, proposedAmount float64, reason string, requesterRole string, requesterClubID *int64) (int64, error) {
43: 	b, err := s.store.GetBudgetByID(budgetID)
44: 	if err != nil {
45: 		return 0, err
46: 	}
47: 	if requesterRole == "team_lead" {
48: 		if requesterClubID == nil {
49: 			return 0, errors.New("team lead missing club assignment")
50: 		}
51: 		if *requesterClubID != b.ClubID {
52: 			return 0, errors.New("forbidden by club scope")
53: 		}
54: 	}
55: 	if requesterRole != "admin" && requesterRole != "organizer" && requesterRole != "team_lead" {
56: 		return 0, errors.New("insufficient role")
57: 	}
58: 	if b.Amount == 0 {
59: 		return 0, errors.New("budget amount cannot be zero")
60: 	}
61: 	if proposedAmount <= 0 {
62: 		return 0, errors.New("proposed amount must be positive")
63: 	}
64: 	changePct := math.Abs((proposedAmount - b.Amount) / b.Amount * 100)
65: 	if changePct <= 10 {
66: 		if requesterRole != "admin" && requesterRole != "organizer" {
67: 			return 0, errors.New("insufficient role")
68: 		}
69: 		if _, err := s.store.DB.Exec(`UPDATE budgets SET amount = ? WHERE id = ?`, proposedAmount, budgetID); err != nil {
70: 			return 0, err
71: 		}
72: 		return 0, nil
73: 	}
74: 	if requesterRole != "organizer" {
75: 		return 0, errors.New(">10% change requests must be submitted by organizer")
76: 	}
77: 	return s.store.InsertBudgetChangeRequest(models.BudgetChangeRequest{
78: 		BudgetID:       budgetID,
79: 		RequestedBy:    requesterID,
80: 		ProposedAmount: proposedAmount,
81: 		ChangePercent:  changePct,
82: 		Reason:         reason,
83: 		Status:         "pending",
84: 	})
85: }
86: 
87: func (s *FinanceService) ListBudgets(clubID *int64) ([]models.Budget, error) {
88: 	return s.store.ListBudgets(clubID)
89: }
90: 
91: func (s *FinanceService) ListPendingChanges(clubID *int64) ([]models.BudgetChangeRequest, error) {
92: 	return s.store.ListPendingBudgetChanges(clubID)
93: }
94: 
95: func (s *FinanceService) RecordSpend(budgetID int64, spent float64) error {
96: 	if spent < 0 {
97: 		return errors.New("spent must be non-negative")
98: 	}
99: 	budget, err := s.store.GetBudgetByID(budgetID)
100: 	if err != nil {
101: 		return err
102: 	}
103: 	if err := s.store.UpdateBudgetSpent(budgetID, spent); err != nil {
104: 		return err
105: 	}
106: 	alert := budget.Amount > 0 && (spent/budget.Amount) >= 0.85
107: 	return s.store.UpdateBudgetThreshold(budgetID, alert)
108: }
109: 
110: func (s *FinanceService) ApproveChange(changeID, adminID int64, approve bool) error {
111: 	requesterID, err := s.store.GetBudgetChangeRequester(changeID)
112: 	if err != nil {
113: 		return err
114: 	}
115: 	if requesterID == adminID {
116: 		return errors.New("reviewer must differ from requester")
117: 	}
118: 	return s.store.ApproveBudgetChange(changeID, adminID, approve)
119: }
120: 
121: func (s *FinanceService) StartThresholdWorker(every time.Duration) {
122: 	ticker := time.NewTicker(every)
123: 	defer ticker.Stop()
124: 	for range ticker.C {
125: 		_ = s.RefreshThresholdAlerts()
126: 	}
127: }
128: 
129: func (s *FinanceService) RefreshThresholdAlerts() error {
130: 	budgets, err := s.store.ListBudgets(nil)
131: 	if err != nil {
132: 		return err
133: 	}
134: 	for _, b := range budgets {
135: 		alert := b.Amount > 0 && (b.Spent/b.Amount) >= 0.85
136: 		if alert != b.ThresholdAlert {
137: 			if err := s.store.UpdateBudgetThreshold(b.ID, alert); err != nil {
138: 				return err
139: 			}
140: 			before := map[string]any{"threshold_alert": b.ThresholdAlert}
141: 			after := map[string]any{"threshold_alert": alert}
142: 			_ = s.store.InsertAuditLog(nil, "WORKER", "/workers/budget-thresholds", "budgets", strconv.FormatInt(b.ID, 10), before, after)
143: 		}
144: 	}
145: 	return nil
146: }
147: 
148: func (s *FinanceService) Projection(b models.Budget, expectedRemainingSpend float64) float64 {
149: 	return b.Amount - (b.Spent + expectedRemainingSpend)
150: }

10: func TestBudgetApprovalWorkflowOverTenPercent(t *testing.T) {
11: 	st := setupStore(t)
12: 	defer st.Close()
13: 	finance := services.NewFinanceService(st)
14: 	auth := services.NewAuthService(st, 30*time.Minute, 5, 15*time.Minute)

17: 		t.Fatal(err)
18: 	}

20: 	if err != nil {
21: 		t.Fatal(err)
22: 	}
23: 	bID, err := finance.CreateBudget(1, "acct-1", "camp-1", "proj-1", "monthly", "2026-03", 1000, 1)
24: 	if err != nil {
25: 		t.Fatal(err)
26: 	}

29: 		t.Fatal(err)
30: 	}
31: 	if changeID == 0 {
32: 		t.Fatalf("expected approval request for >10%% change")

35: 		t.Fatal(err)
36: 	}

78: 		FROM budget_change_requests bcr

80: 		WHERE bcr.status = 'pending'`

97: 			return nil, err

116: 	tx, err := s.DB.Begin()
117: 	if err != nil {
118: 		return err
119: 	}
120: 	defer tx.Rollback()
121: 
122: 	var budgetID int64
123: 	var amount float64
124: 	if err := tx.QueryRow(`SELECT budget_id, proposed_amount FROM budget_change_requests WHERE id = ? AND status = 'pending'`, changeID).Scan(&budgetID, &amount); err != nil {
125: 		return err
126: 	}
127: 	status := "rejected"
128: 	if approve {
129: 		status = "approved"
130: 		if _, err := tx.Exec(`UPDATE budgets SET amount = ? WHERE id = ?`, amount, budgetID); err != nil {
131: 			return err
132: 		}
133: 	}
134: 	if _, err := tx.Exec(`UPDATE budget_change_requests SET status = ?, reviewed_by = ? WHERE id = ?`, status, adminID, changeID); err != nil {
135: 		return err
136: 	}
137: 	return tx.Commit()
138: }

144: 	}

147: 			return false, nil
148: 		}
149: 		if user.ClubID == nil {
150: 			return false, errors.New("club scope required")
151: 		}
152: 		if user.ClubID != nil && b.ClubID != *user.ClubID {
153: 			return false, nil
154: 		}
155: 	}
156: 	return true, nil

content.text:
You were right ΓÇö it was effectively role-limited.

- In `internal/services/finance_service.go`, the `>10%` path previously hard-required `organizer` only, and `<=10%` direct updates also excluded `team_lead`.
- That conflicted with route-level access, where `/api/budgets/:id/change` allows `admin`, `organizer`, and `team_lead`.

I fixed it so the 10% rule now applies consistently to all allowed requester roles:

- `<=10%`: direct update for any authorized role (`admin`/`organizer`/`team_lead`) with existing club-scope checks preserved.
- `>10%`: creates a pending change request for any authorized role (instead of organizer-only).

Updated + verified:

- Code: `internal/services/finance_service.go:65`
- Tests added:
  - `unit_tests/finance_test.go:83` (`admin` over 10% creates request)
  - `unit_tests/finance_test.go:100` (`team_lead` over 10% creates request)
- Validation run:
  - `go test ./unit_tests/... -v` passed
  - `go test ./API_tests/... -run Budget -v` passed

If you want, I can also add API-level tests that explicitly POST `/api/budgets/:id/change` as `admin` and `team_lead` for both `<10%` and `>10%` so this canΓÇÖt regress at handler/integration level.
